Empires & Puzzles Gongniu Review: 5-Star Legendary Holy Yellow Hero from Zodiac Family

Gongniu is a lesson in patience—literally. His skills take 3 turns to kick in, but his passive shuts down minions instantly. Is this Slow Holy hero worth the wait, or is he just pasture ornament? We break down the stats and strategy.
Gongniu Review: The Patient Ox Who Hates Minions
Gongniu: The Zen Master of "Wait for It... Wait for It..."

Gongniu arrived with the Lunar New Year 2024 celebration, representing the Year of the Ox (despite arriving in the Year of the Dragon, Empires & Puzzles time is wibbly-wobbly). He is a Slow mana Holy Cleric designed to be a bastion of team survivability. Unlike the instant gratification of modern heroes who blast enemies into the next dimension upon firing, Gongniu asks you to invest in a 3-turn bond of trust. He promises great rewards—healing and mana—but only if you can survive long enough to cash the check.

Gongniu is a majestic, anthropomorphic ox dressed in traditional festive robes, pouring a magical elixir from a gourd. The art style is vibrant and respectful of the source material, capturing a sense of calm authority. He looks like the kind of guy who would give you really good life advice while crushing a goblin with one hand. It's a refreshing change from the "angry guy with big sword" aesthetic.

When Gongniu was released in early 2024, his stats were considered quite tanky, boasting over 1000 Defense and 1800 Health. However, looking at him through the lens of the current meta (where new heroes are pushing 1300+ Defense and 2200+ Health), he has fallen behind significantly.
While not "paper thin," he relies heavily on his defensive buff to survive against modern snipers who can easily hit for 1000+ damage.
Gongniu's special skill is a unique "delayed gratification" mechanic. Let's break down the three components:

+54% Defense is substantial. The fact that it increases by +5% every time an ally is hit (up to a likely cap, usually +64% or higher) makes your team incredibly sturdy against tile damage and multi-hitters. This is the only "instant" part of his skill.

He promises 1000 HP after 3 turns. If the buff is removed (dispelled or cleansed) or replaced (overwritten by another buff), it triggers early for a massive 1400 HP heal. This is a brilliant counter-play to dispellers. If enemies try to strip your buffs, you get healed
Similar to the heal, you get 25% Mana after 3 turns. If removed/replaced, it jumps to 35%.
Crucially, this effect cannot be replaced with itself.
This prevents infinite mana loops if you fire Gongniu twice in quick succession.

The skill is a gamble. In a fast-paced raid, 3 turns is an eternity. However, the "trap" mechanic (triggering stronger effects on removal) forces the opponent into a difficult decision: suffer through the high defense, or dispel it and fully heal your team.
Slow (12 tiles). With a Lv. 29 Magic/Styx troop or Lv. 30 Legendary troop, you can shave this down, but he will rarely be "fast."
1000 HP is roughly 40-50% of a modern hero's health pool. 1400 HP is nearly a full heal for many heroes. This is one of the strongest raw heals in the game, balanced by the delay.
35% mana is roughly 3.5 to 4 tiles worth of mana. That is a massive swing that can instantly charge Average or Fast heroes from empty to full if they had some charge already.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Zodiac family (Lunar New Year) is somewhat disjointed, but Gongniu brings some specific utility.
The bonus depends on which other Lunar heroes are present. It's a nice cherry on top but rarely a strategy to build around given the rarity of these heroes.
This passive is arguably more valuable than his special skill. It completely shuts down the special effects of enemy minions.
M&M or Lady of the Lake
It does not remove the minions or stop them from acting as meat shields, but it neuters their utility.
Recovering 10% health when receiving an ailment is fantastic for a tank. It gives him passive sustainability against DoT heavy teams.
At the start of battle, regenerates boosted health for 6 turns. This helps offset his Slow speed, keeping him alive long enough to fire his first cast.

Upon release, Gongniu was seen as a "Rush War specialist" and a niche counter to the minion meta. He wasn't the flashy damage dealer everyone wanted (like his portal-mate Qinglong), but smart players recognized the value of his passive.

His stats have aged poorly. A 5-star hero with sub-900 Attack and barely 1000 Defense struggles to stand on the front lines against 2025/2026 monsters. His healing numbers (1000/1400) are fixed, meaning they don't scale with stats, but they remain high enough to be relevant.
Gongniu is not a hero you take into every raid. However, in
wars/tournaments, he is still S-Tier. His passive also keeps him relevant as a hard counter to minion-heavy defenses, even if you just hide him in the wing to keep the passive active.
You are using Gongniu to survive and cast his spell. He deals zero damage with his skill. Do not give him Attack nodes.
Maximizes his survivability. Every point of Defense makes his +54% buff more effective.
His tile damage will be negligible.
. He is a Cleric, so his Manashield talent (chance to resist mana effects) pairs beautifully with his role as a cleanser/healer. Keeping him mana-active is priority number one.
Just bringing him makes Waterpipe and other minion pests significantly less annoying.
Punishes enemies for dispelling your buffs by instantly healing and granting mana.
In Very Fast modes, the 3-turn delay matters less because he charges so quickly, and the mana boost helps cycle your other slow heroes.
Outside of Rush modes, 12 tiles is a lot to ask for a hero that doesn't do anything immediately (except buff defense).
If he fires and then your team gets wiped out on the next turn, his healing does nothing.
If he gets hit by Buff Block (e.g., from Hippo or generic blockers), he does literally nothing. His skill is 100% buff-based.
He is squishy compared to modern heroes.
This is his home. He is an absolute monster here.
The high defense and massive healing make auto-farming safer.
Bringing him specifically to counter minion-heavy teams.
Gongniu (Tank/Flank) + Cleanse/Dispel Punisher (e.g., Costumed Snow White)
Gongniu buffs the team. If the enemy dispels, you get healed. If they don't, you have +54% defense. Pair him with heroes like

the 1400 Heal and 35% Mana boost instantly for the whole team. It's a devastating combo.
Mother North - Gongniu - Khufu - Alfrike - Alberich
A classic Rush defense. Gongniu keeps the revivers alive with defense buffs. If he fires, the mana boost helps Alfrike and Khufu fire again.
Heroes who prevent buffs (e.g., Mist, Onyx) render Gongniu useless.
While stealing counts as "removing" (triggering the heal), the enemy still gets your defense buff.
Modern heavy hitters might just kill him through the defense buff before the heal triggers.

If you pulled Gongniu early, he is a fantastic safety net. He will keep your team alive through difficult map stages and quests. Level him up, but be aware that he might feel "slow" in raids until you get better troops.

Gongniu is a situational tool. Don't strip him of emblems if you play Rush wars competitively. However, don't prioritize Limit Breaking him (LB2) unless you specifically need him for your top war defense. His stats are his biggest weakness, so if you use him, you must protect him.
When released, the community was torn.
predicting the annoyance of his defense buff.
Others were skeptical of the mechanics:
"He’s slow to charge, then you have to wait three more turns... Not too useful IMO."
was frequent, with players testing if the heal triggered on death (it generally doesn't, making timing critical).
The consensus settled on him being a
—carried largely by his ability to shut down minions.

Gongniu is the definition of a "specialist." In the wild west of modern Empires & Puzzles, bringing a slow hero with delayed skills to a gunfight seems crazy. But when the conditions are right—specifically in Rush Attack or against minion hordes—this Ox is strong as, well, an ox. He isn't flashy, he isn't fast, but he is one of the few heroes who can turn an enemy's dispel into your advantage.
Keep him for Rush wars and Minion counters, but don't expect him to be your daily driver in the current speed meta.

A detailed review and strategy guide for Gongniu, the Holy 5-star hero from the Zodiac family. We analyze his stats, delayed healing mechanics, and his powerful anti-minion passive in the current meta.
